title: Elegies: Trust No One
description: Theognis of Megara on the corruption of values, the unreliability of friends, and survival in a declining age.
introduction: The elegies of Theognis preserve the worldview of the archaic Greek aristocracy in its twilight: a world where old values are being overturned, new money corrupts old blood, and the poet can trust no one. These poems were used as a school text throughout antiquity.

translation.original_text: μηδένα τῶνδε φίλον ποιεῦ, Πολυπαΐδη, ἀστῶν ἐκ θυμοῦ χρείης εἵνεκα μηδεμιῆς.
translation.text: Make none of these citizens your true friend, son of Polypaos, not from the heart, not for any need.
